description: Condition that causes a flow to be executed.

Schedule.description: Triggers the flow on a cron schedule.

cron.description: Cron5 expression defining the schedule e.g. `@daily` or `*/30 * * * *`.

Event.description: Triggers the flow when an event bus event matching one of the filters is observed.

events.description: Filters the event by type and fields.

cooldown.description: The trigger will fire upon first observed event. If another event arrives withing the `cooldown` interval the firing will be postponed until `cooldown` interval ends. I.e. trigger is guaranteed to fire, but may batch multiple events together into one flow run.

cooldownMaxBatch.description: If an event is observed a `cooldownMaxBatch` number of times during the `cooldown` interval it will fire the trigger without waiting for cooldown to finish.

Source.description: Triggers the flow when a source receives new data, with optional batching controls.

source.description: Reference to the source resource that drives this trigger.

minRecordsToAwait.description: Minimum number of new records to accumulate before triggering.

maxAwaitInterval.description: Maximum time to wait for `minRecordsToAwait` before triggering anyway e.g. `1h`.

Dataset.description: Triggers the flow when matching datasets are updated.

dataset.description: Selector that identifies which datasets can trigger this flow.

events.description: Set of event bus event IDs that this trigger will react to
